name: Thai Chicken with Basil
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Skinless, boneless chicken - breast cut into fingernail-size cubes
1 tbsp Minced fresh chili pepper (serrano or jalapeno)
2 tbsp Fish sauce (nam pla or nuoc mam, available in oriental markets)
1 medium Red sweet bell pepper - cut into thin strips
1 medium Carrot - cut into thin strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut chicken into fingernail-size cubes and set aside.
2. Chop garlic and mince chili pepper, then set aside.
3. Cut bell pepper and carrots into thin strips.
4. Mix fish sauce, soy sauce, and water together in a small bowl and set aside.
5. Measure sugar; set aside.
6. Heat wok or deep skillet over medium-high heat. Add the oil and swirl to coat the pan.
7. Drop a piece of garlic into the hot oil. If it sizzles immediately, the oil is ready.
8. Add the rest of the chopped garlic and toss briefly in the hot oil.
9. Add minced chili pepper and toss until the garlic begins to turn golden, about 15 seconds.
10. Add cubed chicken to the pan and stir-fry until the flesh changes color, about 1 minute.
11. Add the fish sauce mixture and stir to combine with the meat for about 1 minute.
12. Add sugar and stir for another 30 seconds.
13. Add basil leaves and the cut bell pepper and carrot strips, then toss.
14. Stir-fry until the basil begins to wilt.
15. Transfer the dish to a serving bowl and serve hot. Makes 2 portions.
Adapted From Washington Post, "Real Thai" by Nancie McDermott
